class.name: GIFWriter
class.brief_description: Writes GIF files from image data.
class.description: GIFWriter provides methods to create GIF files from [Image] objects. Supports both incremental frame-by-frame writing and one-shot complete GIF creation. Features include customizable frame delays, loop counts, transparency, disposal methods, and color quantization.

method.name: add_comment
method.description: Adds a comment block to the GIF. Must be called after setting canvas size but before closing.

method.name: begin_frame
method.description: Begins writing a new frame with the specified position and dimensions. Must be followed by [method write_frame_pixels] or [method write_frame_image], then [method end_frame].

method.name: close
method.description: Closes the file or buffer and completes the GIF writing. Must be called to finalize the output.

method.name: end_frame
method.description: Ends the current frame writing. Must be called after writing frame data before beginning another frame.

method.name: get_canvas_size
method.description: Returns the canvas size set by [method set_canvas_size].

method.name: get_frame_count
method.description: Returns the number of frames written so far.

method.name: get_output_buffer
method.description: Returns the memory buffer containing the GIF data. Only valid after calling [method open_from_buffer] and [method close].

method.name: open
method.description: Opens a file for writing. The file will be overwritten if it exists.

method.name: open_from_buffer
method.description: Opens a memory buffer for writing. Use [method get_output_buffer] to retrieve the result after closing.

method.name: save_to_buffer
method.description: Static method to write a GIF to a memory buffer. Returns a Dictionary with [code]error[/code] (GIFError) and [code]data[/code] (PackedByteArray).

method.name: save_to_file
method.description: Static method to save a GIF file from an array of images. Delays are specified in milliseconds.

method.name: set_canvas_size
method.description: Sets the canvas size (screen descriptor). Must be called before writing the first frame and can only be called once.

method.name: set_color_resolution
method.description: Sets the color resolution in bits (1-8, default 8). Must be called before writing frames.

method.name: set_disposal_method
method.description: Sets the disposal method for the current frame.

method.name: set_frame_delay
method.description: Sets the delay time in milliseconds for the current frame.

method.name: set_frame_palette
method.description: Sets the local color palette for the current frame. Maximum 256 colors.

method.name: set_global_palette
method.description: Sets the global color palette. If not set, each frame must have its own local palette.

method.name: set_loop_count
method.description: Sets the loop count using the Netscape application extension. 0 means infinite loop.

method.name: set_transparent_color
method.description: Sets the transparent color index for the current frame. Use -1 for no transparency.

method.name: write_frame_image
method.description: Writes frame data from an [Image] object. Automatically quantizes colors if [param quantize] is true.

method.name: write_frame_pixels
method.description: Writes raw pixel indices for the current frame. Data length must equal width * height.

method.name: write_gif
method.description: One-shot method to write all frames. Must be called after opening a file or buffer and setting canvas size.

constants.constant: Failed to close the file.
Pixel data size exceeds width * height.
Restore to background color before rendering next frame.
Do not dispose, keep current frame visible.
Restore to previous frame before rendering next frame.
Disposal method not specified.
Write failed, disk may be full.
Image descriptor is already active.
Screen descriptor already set, attempted to set second one.
Invalid dimensions (0 or exceeds 65535).
No global or local color map set.
No file is currently open.
No image descriptor set.
No screen descriptor set.
Not enough memory to allocate required structures.
File is not open for writing.
Failed to open the specified file.
Palette contains more than 256 colors.
Operation completed successfully.
Failed to write to file.
